target.name: Porotech
target.founder: Tongtong Zhu, Yingjun Liu, Rachel Oliver

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Porotech is a Series A chip design company in the Semiconductors sector. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public comparables in the same space, including Series A peers Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, and Rain AI.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression uses the $20M+ total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A progression, implying $40M. Peer-Median anchors to four same-sector, same-stage private companies: Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M), yielding a median of $1.5B with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via Semiconductors P/S multiples (24.4x median across ALAB, NVDA, AMD) is not applicable; Porotech's revenue is not disclosed.

The triangulated valuation range is $34M to $1.7B, with a median of $770M across the two applicable methods. The extreme width of this range reflects material dispersion in the peer group, where same-stage chip/semiconductor companies trade at valuations spanning 10x. Key uncertainties include Porotech's differentiation within the crowded frontier-chip landscape, technology maturity, and capital efficiency relative to the peer set. The low end anchors conservatively to deployment-stage progression; the high end reflects elevated valuations in the peer universe but should be weighted heavily only with evidence of comparable technical or market traction.
